findings.description: flush() constructs a new smtplib.SMTP connection (TCP connect + EHLO handshake) and quit()s it per message inside the loop — O(n) connections per flush instead of one reused session, adding ~50-200ms overhead per email and risking relay rate-limiting; hoist a single 'with smtplib.SMTP(...) as server' outside the loop.
smtplib.SMTP is created without a timeout, so a hung or unreachable SMTP server blocks flush() indefinitely and stalls the entire pipeline stage; pass an explicit timeout.

_seen is an unbounded dedup cache with no TTL or max size that flush() never clears (line 65 only clears _pending), so a long-running pipeline process accumulates every recipient ever enqueued — a slow memory leak; clear on flush or bound the set.

A single sendmail exception escapes flush() before _pending.clear() runs, leaving already-sent messages queued for duplicate re-send on retry (and losing per-message error isolation) — defer detail to critic-logic.
Dedup key is recipient address only and survives flush, contradicting the 'duplicate already queued' docstring and silently dropping distinct messages to the same recipient — defer to critic-logic.
_render interpolates msg.to and msg.subject unsanitized into raw SMTP headers, enabling CRLF header/body injection — defer to critic-security.
Plaintext SMTP on submission port 587 with no STARTTLS or authentication — defer to critic-security.
No synchronization on _pending/_seen, so concurrent enqueue/flush from multiple pipeline workers would race (lost or duplicated sends) — speculative since no multithreaded caller is in scope.
MIMEText is imported and the 'email' logger (line 9) is created but neither is ever used — dead code.
Message.template field is never consumed by _render/flush/preview — dead field.
